title: Feng Shui Basics — The Art of Harmonious Placement
summary: Feng Shui, meaning "wind and water," is the ancient Chinese practice of arranging space to create harmony with the environment.
content:
Feng Shui (风水) is the traditional Chinese art of spatial arrangement, based on the idea that our surroundings affect our wellbeing. Its goal is to create harmony between people and their environment.

【Core Concept: Qi】
Qi (气) is the vital energy that flows through all things. Good Feng Shui aims to "collect the wind and hold the water" — to allow qi to flow gently without stagnating or escaping.

【Two Major Schools】
1. Form School (形势): Focuses on the physical features of the landscape — mountains (dragon), hills (sand), water, and the direction they face.
2. Compass School (理气): Uses the Eight Trigrams and Five Elements to calculate favorable directions based on time and space.

【The Three Essentials of a House】
- The Door: Where qi enters. Should be clear, well-lit, and unobstructed.
- The Bedroom: Where you spend a third of your life. Headboard against a wall for support.
- The Kitchen: Where nourishment is created. Should be clean and well-organized.

【Modern Perspective】
Many Feng Shui principles are actually ancient observations about environmental psychology and architecture. Whether or not you believe in qi, creating a calm, organized living space has proven psychological benefits.

title: Feng Shui Layout Guide for Each Room in a House
summary: Different rooms have different Feng Shui considerations; a reasonable layout can bring a harmonious home atmosphere.
content:
The core of home Feng Shui is to allow 'Qi' to flow smoothly within the home without being lost. Different rooms have different considerations.

【Main Door Feng Shui】
- The area in front of the main door should be open and bright, without obstructions
- The main door should not directly face an elevator or staircase
- The main door should not directly face a bathroom or kitchen door
- It is advisable to set up a foyer inside the main door as a buffer

【Living Room Feng Shui】
- The living room should be spacious and bright, with the sofa preferably against a wall
- A square-shaped living room is ideal; avoid having a beam pressing down from above

【Bedroom Feng Shui】
- The bed should not directly face the door or a mirror
- The head of the bed should be against a wall for support
- The bedroom should not be too large or too small
- Colors should be soft, not too bright

【Kitchen Feng Shui】
- The kitchen should not be located in the center of the house
- The stove should not directly face the refrigerator (Water and Fire clash)
- The kitchen must be kept clean and tidy

【Bathroom Feng Shui】
- The bathroom should not be located in the center of the house
- It must be kept ventilated and dry

The highest level of Feng Shui is to make people feel comfortable and natural. A home that makes you feel relaxed and happy is the best Feng Shui.
